title: Ultra-long read sequencing protocol for Oxford Nanopore
author: Josh Quick
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.k88czzw
materials: Leave overnight at 5°C before use. Undetermined
Using a thermocycler incubate at 30°C for 1 minute, 80°C for 1 minute then hold at 4°C. Undetermined 00:01:00 Undetermined 00:01:00 Undetermined, hold

steps.title: Take 5x10 7 human cell pellet...
steps.instruction: Take 5x10 7 human cell pellet fresh or stored at -80°C in a 50 ml Falcon tube (for E. coli I used an overnight culture of E. coli growing in 50 ml LB broth spun down at 4500 x g for 10 minutes) DNA extraction protocol adapted from Molecular Cloning by Sambrook and Russell (third edition) Chapter 6 protocol 1.

steps.title: Resuspend by pipette mixing in 200 µl sterile PBS
steps.instruction: Resuspend by pipette mixing in 200 µl sterile PBS. undetermined, PBS 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline )

steps.title: Add 10 ml TLB and vortex...
steps.instruction: Add 10 ml TLB and vortex at full speed for 5 seconds. undetermined, TLB 00:00:05 Vortexing here is to thoughly resuspend the cells to achieve a homogenous DNA solution once cells are lysed, DNA will not be damaged as it is still packaged inside the cell.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour. Undetermined 01:00:00 Solution will turn transparent as the cells lyse.

steps.title: Add 100 µl Qiagen Proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl Qiagen Proteinase K or other stock solution to a final concentration of 200 µg/ml. Mix by slowly rotating end-over-end 3 times. undetermined, Proteinase K Proteinase K (2 ml) Qiagen Catalog #19131

steps.title: Incubate at 50°C for 2 hours,...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50°C for 2 hours, mix every 30 minutes by slowly rotating end-over-end 3 times. Undetermined 02:00:00

steps.title: Split the viscous lysate into the...
steps.instruction: Split the viscous lysate into the two 15 ml Falcon tubes prepared with phase-lock gel, this is easiest using a 10 ml serological pipette at slow speed.

steps.title: Add 5 ml recently opened BioUltra...
steps.instruction: Add 5 ml recently opened BioUltra TE-saturated phenol to each Falcon tube containing lysate. undetermined, TE-saturated phenol BioUltra TE-saturated phenol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #77607

steps.title: Place on a HulaMixer at 20...
steps.instruction: Place on a HulaMixer at 20 rpm for 10 minutes, if a fine emulsion has not formed after a minute gradually increase the rotation speed. 00:10:00

steps.title: Spin in a centrifuge at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin in a centrifuge at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes. 00:10:00

steps.title: Pour the aqueous phases into two...
steps.instruction: Pour the aqueous phases into two new 15 ml Falcon tubes containing phase-lock gel, try to avoid transferring any protein which may form a white layer above the phase-lock gel.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 ml buffer saturated phenol and 2.5 ml chloroform-isoamyl alcohol 24:1 to each tube. undetermined, TE-saturated phenol BioUltra TE-saturated phenol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #77607 undetermined, Chloroform-Isoamyl Alcohol Chloroform-Isoamyl Alcohol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #25666

steps.title: Place on a HulaMixer at 20...
steps.instruction: Place on a HulaMixer at 20 rpm for 10 minutes, if a fine emulsion has not formed after a minute gradually increase the rotation speed. 00:10:00

steps.title: Spin in a centrifuge at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin in a centrifuge at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes. 00:10:00

steps.title: Combine the aqueous phases from the...
steps.instruction: Combine the aqueous phases from the two tubes by pouring slowly into a new 50 ml Falcon tube.

steps.title: Add 4 ml 5 M ammonium acetate
steps.instruction: Add 4 ml 5 M ammonium acetate. undetermined, Ammonium Acetate 100ml Ammonium Acetate [5M] G-Biosciences Catalog #R012

steps.title: Add 30 ml ice-cold ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Add 30 ml ice-cold ethanol and watch the DNA precipitate, bubbles will over time pull the mass of DNA to the surface so it looks like a Jellyfish with tentacles hanging down. undetermined Ethanol absolute Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #107017

steps.title: Make a hook by melting the...
steps.instruction: Make a hook by melting the tip of glass capillary in a blue flame so it curls over.

steps.title: Hook out the DNA in one-piece...
steps.instruction: Hook out the DNA in one-piece if possible lift up and allow the excess liquid to drip off.

steps.title: Submerge the DNA in a 50...
steps.instruction: Submerge the DNA in a 50 ml Falcon tube containing 70% ethanol. It should have fully tightened up into a whitish opaque pellet.

steps.title: Carefully work the pellet off the...
steps.instruction: Carefully work the pellet off the glass rod using the rim of an Eppendorf tube, let the pellet drop into the tube.

steps.title: Go back for the rest of...
steps.instruction: Go back for the rest of the DNA if it broke apart and repeat.

steps.title: Add 1 ml 70% ethanol to the Eppendorf
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml 70% ethanol to the Eppendorf. undetermined, 70% ethanol

steps.title: Spin down at 10,000 xg then...
steps.instruction: Spin down at 10,000 xg then remove as much of the 70% ethanol as possible.

steps.title: Wash again with 1 ml 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash again with 1 ml 70% ethanol. undetermined, 70% ethanol

steps.title: Let the remaining ethanol evaporate by...
steps.instruction: Let the remaining ethanol evaporate by leaving at RT for 15 minutes. 00:15:00 Undetermined

steps.title: Add 100 µl EB + Triton-X100...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl EB + Triton-X100 and incubate without mixing at 5°C for 2 days to allow the pellet to fully resuspend into a translucent viscous gel. undetermined, EB+Triton-X100 48:00:00 We have found that adding Triton-X100 to a final concentration of 0.02% dramatically improves the transposase activity. If you don't want to add it to the stock DNA you can add it when you make the library. Undetermined

steps.title: Take a P2 pipette set to 1
steps.instruction: Take a P2 pipette set to 1.5 µl with a cut-off tip and aspirate very slowly then try to work the DNA in the tip away from the DNA in the tube by continuously pulling and releasing the DNA to work it gradually away. Adjust the pipette volume to determine the volume of DNA you actually removed.

steps.title: Quantify the DNA on the Qubit...
steps.instruction: Quantify the DNA on the Qubit BR assay, the concentration should be >1 µg/µl, it is important to use BR as the buffer contains a detergent which appears to improve mixing.

steps.title: Check the absorbance spectrum of the...
steps.instruction: Check the absorbance spectrum of the 1:10 diluted DNA on the NanoDrop, example trace below (ratios typically a little higher than other methods) a

steps.title: If necessary add more EB+Triton-X100 to...
steps.instruction: If necessary add more EB+Triton-X100 to the stock DNA to adjust concentration to 1 µg/ul but do not mix.

steps.title: As slowly as you can pipette...
steps.instruction: As slowly as you can pipette 16 µl DNA into a 0.2 ml PCR tube using a cut-off P20 pipette tip, retain the tip. undetermined, DNA

steps.title: Remove 1 µl from the tube...
steps.instruction: Remove 1 µl from the tube as before and quantify using Qubit BR assay. undetermined DNA may still be heterogenous in concentration and as such this is the most reliable way to quantify actually input.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µl FRA and 3.5 µl EB+Triton-X100. Using a P20 set to 18 µl and the tip from before, mix up and down as slowly as possible 8 times, retain the tip. undetermined, FRA undetermined, EB+Triton-X100 Take care not to introduce bubbles as they are hard to remove. We have found that 1.5 µl FRA is suffient to generate an efficient library and any more can lead to over-fragmentation.

steps.title: Add 1 µl RAP
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl RAP. Using a P20 set to 19 µl and the tip from before, mix up and down as slowly as possible 8 times, discard tip. undetermined, RAP

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature while you prime the flowcell
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature while you prime the flowcell.

steps.title: Add 30 µl FLT to tube...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µl FLT to tube of FLB, vortex briefly and spin down. This is the flush mix. undetermined, FLT

steps.title: Place new flowcell on MinION and run platform QC
steps.instruction: Place new flowcell on MinION and run platform QC .

steps.title: Using a P1000 remove a little...
steps.instruction: Using a P1000 remove a little storage buffer from the inlet port using the volume adjustment screw. Load 800 µl flush mix via the inlet port slowly using the plunger. Wait 5 minutes. undetermined, Flush mix 00:05:00

steps.title: Lift the cover off the SpotON port
steps.instruction: Lift the cover off the SpotON port. Load 200 µl flush mix via the inlet port slowly using the plunger, try to dispense at a speed where a bead of liquid becomes visible over the SpotON port which then gets siphoned back in. undetermined, Flush mix

steps.title: Add 34 µl SQT and 20...
steps.instruction: Add 34 µl SQT and 20 µl NFW to the library tube. Loading beads are not used as they clump when mixed with the library.

steps.title: Using a P100 set to 75...
steps.instruction: Using a P100 set to 75 µl with a cut-off tip mix up and down as slowly as possible 5 times. On the final mix slowly pipette the diluted library onto the SpotON port as it gets siphoned in, this can take much longer than usual due to the viscosity. If it gets blocked abandon the siphon and load by holding the pipette vertically against the SpotON port and positively pipetting the library directly in.

steps.title: Add light phase-lock gel to 2 x 15 ml Falcons
steps.instruction: Add light phase-lock gel to 2 x 15 ml Falcons. If it is only available in 2 ml tubes, transfer it by cutting the lid off 3 x 2 ml tubes and spinning it out into each 15 ml Falcon. 15 ml Falcons are used as they are narrower decreasing the surface area of the interface/gel. Using two means they balance each other in the centrifuge and gives phenol space to move which improves the emulsion.

steps.title: Spin down at 10,000 xg then...
steps.instruction: Spin down at 10,000 xg then remove as much of the 70% ethanol as possible.
